metadata.description: Transformer: Transformers utilize self-attention mechanisms to process sequences and form the foundational architecture of large models like BERT and GPT. Python code example (a simple self-attention mechanism implementation): ```python import torch import torch.nn as nn class SelfAttention(nn.Module): def __init__(self, embed_dim, num_heads=8): super(SelfAttention, self).__init__() self.embed_dim = embed_dim self.num_heads = num_heads self.head_dim = embed_dim // num_heads self.q_linear = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim) self.v_linear = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim) self.k_linear = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim) def forward(self, x): batch_size, seq_length, embed_dim = x.size() # Linear transformations queries = self.q_linear(x).view(batch_size, seq_length, self.num_heads, self.head_dim).transpose(1, 2) keys = self.k_linear(x).view(batch_size, seq_length, self.num_heads, self.head_dim).transpose(1, 2) values = self.v_linear(x).view(batch_size, seq_length, self.num_heads, self.head_dim).transpose(1, 2) # Scaled dot-product attention energy = torch.matmul(queries / (self.head_dim ** 0.5), keys.transpose(-2, -1)) attention_weights = torch.softmax(energy, dim=-1) # Contextualized output out = torch.matmul(attention_weights, values).transpose(1, 2).contiguous().view(batch_size, seq_length, embed_dim) return out # Example usage: x = torch.randn(32, 50, 512) # Batch size x Sequence length x Embedding dimension self_att = SelfAttention(embed_dim=512) output = self_att(x) print(output.shape) # Expected output shape: (32, 50, 512) ``` This code snippet defines a simple self-attention mechanism class in PyTorch and demonstrates its usage.
